Organization.title: Organization
Organization.description: Represents a client company or brand that utilizes the AssistLink platform, managing its chat widgets, agents, and customer interactions.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Organization entity.

name.description: The name of the organization or company.

websiteUrl.description: The primary website URL for the organization, used for AI-powered color extraction and branding.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the organization record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the organization record was last updated.

User.title: User
User.description: Represents a human user of the AssistLink platform, such as an agent or administrator, associated with a specific organization. This entity is linked to an external authentication system (e.g., Supabase).

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity, typically provided by the authentication system.

organizationId.description: Reference to the Organization this user belongs to. (Relationship: Organization 1:N User)

email.description: The user's email address, used for identification and communication.

firstName.description: The first name of the user.

lastName.description: The last name of the user.

role.description: The role of the user within the organization (e.g., 'admin', 'agent').

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user account was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user account was last updated.

ChatWidgetConfiguration.title: ChatWidgetConfiguration
ChatWidgetConfiguration.description: Defines the visual and behavioral settings for a customizable chat widget deployed by an Organization on their website or application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ChatWidgetConfiguration entity.

organizationId.description: Reference to the Organization that owns this chat widget configuration. (Relationship: Organization 1:N ChatWidgetConfiguration)

name.description: A descriptive name for this widget configuration (e.g., 'Main Website Chat', 'Support Page Widget').

primaryColor.description: The primary color of the chat widget, typically in hex format (e.g., '#3333CC').

backgroundColor.description: The background color of the chat widget, typically in hex format (e.g., '#F0F0F4').

accentColor.description: The accent color for interactive elements and highlights, typically in hex format (e.g., '#1FBAF5').

poweredByAssistLink.description: Indicates whether the 'Powered by AssistLink' branding should be displayed on the widget (typically true for free tiers).

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the widget configuration was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the widget configuration was last updated.

Customer.title: Customer
Customer.description: Represents an individual end-user who interacts with a chat widget. This entity captures basic information about the customer for a specific organization's interactions.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Customer entity. This might be a session ID or a persistent ID if available.

organizationId.description: Reference to the Organization whose chat widget this customer interacted with. (Relationship: Organization 1:N Customer)

widgetConfigurationId.description: Reference to the specific ChatWidgetConfiguration through which this customer initiated a chat. (Relationship: ChatWidgetConfiguration 1:N Customer)

externalId.description: An optional identifier for the customer from the client's own system (e.g., CRM user ID).

name.description: The name provided by the customer during the chat interaction, if available.

email.description: The email address provided by the customer, if available.

lastSeenAt.description: Timestamp of the last known interaction or activity of the customer.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the customer record was first created.

ChatSession.title: ChatSession
ChatSession.description: Represents a single, continuous conversation instance between a customer and one or more support agents.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ChatSession entity.

organizationId.description: Reference to the Organization to which this chat session belongs. (Relationship: Organization 1:N ChatSession)

customerId.description: Reference to the Customer participating in this chat session. (Relationship: Customer 1:N ChatSession)

widgetConfigurationId.description: Reference to the ChatWidgetConfiguration through which this session was initiated. (Relationship: ChatWidgetConfiguration 1:N ChatSession)

agentId.description: Reference to the User (agent) currently assigned to or last interacting with this session. Can be null if unassigned. (Relationship: User 1:N ChatSession)

status.description: The current status of the chat session (e.g., 'open', 'pending', 'resolved', 'closed').

startedAt.description: Timestamp when the chat session began.

endedAt.description: Timestamp when the chat session was ended or resolved.

resolutionNotes.description: Optional notes provided by the agent regarding the resolution of the chat.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the chat session record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the chat session record was last updated.

ChatMessage.title: ChatMessage
ChatMessage.description: Represents an individual message exchanged within a specific chat session.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ChatMessage entity.

chatSessionId.description: Reference to the ChatSession this message belongs to. (Relationship: ChatSession 1:N ChatMessage)

senderId.description: Identifier of the sender (either a UserId or CustomerId). Use with senderType to determine entity.

senderType.description: Specifies whether the sender is an 'agent' (User) or 'customer' (Customer).

content.description: The actual text content of the message.

timestamp.description: Timestamp when the message was sent.

AgentSmartReply.title: AgentSmartReply
AgentSmartReply.description: Stores predefined quick replies that agents can use during chat interactions to increase efficiency.

id.description: Unique identifier for the AgentSmartReply entity.

organizationId.description: Reference to the Organization that owns this smart reply. Can be null if it's a global reply. (Relationship: Organization 1:N AgentSmartReply)

title.description: A short, descriptive title for the smart reply (e.g., 'Greeting', 'FAQ Shipping').

content.description: The full text content of the smart reply.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the smart reply was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the smart reply was last updated.

EmailNotificationSetting.title: EmailNotificationSetting
EmailNotificationSetting.description: Stores preferences for email notifications related to chat events for an organization or individual users.

id.description: Unique identifier for the EmailNotificationSetting entity.

organizationId.description: Reference to the Organization for which these settings apply. (Relationship: Organization 1:N EmailNotificationSetting)

userId.description: Optional reference to a specific User if these are personal notification settings. Overrides organization-wide settings. (Relationship: User 1:N EmailNotificationSetting)

notifyOnNewChat.description: Boolean indicating whether to send an email notification for a new incoming chat.

notifyOnAgentAssignment.description: Boolean indicating whether to send an email notification when a chat is assigned to an agent.

recipientEmail.description: The email address where notifications should be sent, if different from the user's primary email.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the notification setting was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the notification setting was last updated.

definition.description: Top-level collection for client companies using the AssistLink platform. The `organizationId` parameter is central for all nested resource authorization.

User profiles for platform administrators and agents. Each document's ID (`userId`) corresponds to the Supabase authenticated user ID. Includes denormalized `organizationId` and `role` for authorization.
Customizable chat widget settings owned by a specific organization. Includes denormalized `organizationId` for authorization independence.

params.name: organizationId
params.description: The unique identifier of the owning organization.

params.name: widgetId
params.description: The unique identifier of the chat widget configuration.

definition.description: Records of individual end-users (customers) who interact with an organization's chat widgets. Includes denormalized `organizationId` for authorization independence.

params.name: organizationId
params.description: The unique identifier of the organization the customer interacted with.

params.name: customerId
params.description: The unique identifier for the customer.

definition.description: Individual chat conversation instances. Includes denormalized `organizationId`, `customerId`, `widgetConfigurationId`, and `agentId` for comprehensive authorization independence.

params.name: organizationId
params.description: The unique identifier of the organization to which the chat session belongs.

params.name: sessionId
params.description: The unique identifier for the chat session.

definition.description: Individual messages within a chat session. CRITICAL: For Authorization Independence, each document MUST include denormalized `organizationId` and `customerId` (from the parent session) in addition to its existing fields (`chatSessionId`, `senderId`, `senderType`, etc.).

params.name: organizationId
params.description: The unique identifier of the organization to which the chat session belongs.

params.name: sessionId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent chat session.

params.name: messageId
params.description: The unique identifier for the chat message.

definition.description: Predefined quick replies available to agents within a specific organization. Includes denormalized `organizationId` for authorization independence.

params.name: organizationId
params.description: The unique identifier of the organization that owns this smart reply.

params.name: replyId
params.description: The unique identifier for the agent smart reply.

definition.description: Predefined quick replies universally available to all agents, not scoped to a specific organization. Access controlled by general user roles.

params.name: replyId
params.description: The unique identifier for the global agent smart reply.

definition.description: Organization-wide email notification preferences. Includes denormalized `organizationId` for authorization independence. If `userId` is present, it indicates a user's specific override, but these documents are primarily for organization-level defaults.

params.name: organizationId
params.description: The unique identifier of the organization these settings apply to.

params.name: settingId
params.description: The unique identifier for the notification setting (e.g., 'default').

definition.description: Personal email notification preferences for a specific user. Includes denormalized `userId` and `organizationId` (from user's profile) for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user these settings belong to.

params.name: settingId
params.description: The unique identifier for the notification setting (e.g., 'default').

firestore.reasoning:
The proposed Firestore structure prioritizes Authorization Independence and Structural Segregation to ensure robust, debuggable, and scalable security rules. Each document contains all necessary authorization context, eliminating the need for `get()` calls in security rules.

**Authorization Independence via Denormalization:**
*   **Organization Context:** For all subcollections directly or indirectly under `/organizations/{organizationId}`, the `organizationId` is explicitly denormalized into each document (e.g., `ChatWidgetConfiguration`, `Customer`, `ChatSession`, `AgentSmartReply`, `EmailNotificationSetting`). This allows rules to verify ownership and organizational scope without fetching the parent organization document.
*   **User/Customer Context:** `User` documents include `organizationId` and `role`. `ChatSession` documents denormalize `customerId` and `agentId`. Crucially, for `ChatMessage` documents, the `organizationId` and `customerId` of the parent `ChatSession` must also be denormalized directly into each `ChatMessage` document. This ensures that rules can determine sender/receiver identity and organizational context at the message level, preventing rules from needing to `get()` the parent `ChatSession`.

**QAPs (Queries as Authorization Policies):**
*   **Structural Segregation:** Data with different security postures is stored in separate collections or subcollections. For instance, global `AgentSmartReply` documents are separate from organization-specific ones, allowing distinct access rules.
*   **Path-Based Security:** User-specific data (e.g., `/users/{userId}/emailNotificationSettings`) is stored in paths owned by the `userId` in the path, making it simple to write rules that grant access only to the authenticated user (`request.auth.uid`).
*   **Denormalized Fields for Lists:** By including `organizationId` and `customerId` directly in documents like `ChatSession` and `ChatMessage`, queries for lists (e.g., "all chat sessions for my organization" or "all my messages in a session") can be directly filtered by these fields, and security rules can validate these filters efficiently without resorting to `get()` or insecure `list` operations. For example, an agent can `list` `/organizations/{organizationId}/chatSessions` by filtering `organizationId` to match their own, and the rules can enforce `resource.data.organizationId == request.auth.token.organizationId`.

**Handling User Request Ambiguity:**
*   The request "saving of datas should be to email addresses" is interpreted as ensuring email functionality and proper storage of email addresses within relevant entities (e.g., `User.email`, `Customer.email`, `EmailNotificationSetting.recipientEmail`), rather than using email addresses as document IDs or a primary storage mechanism. Firestore is the primary database. End-to-end encryption is an application-level concern and does not directly dictate the data structure but the content of the fields.
